title: primula calderiana var. strumosa
intro: Primula calderiana var. strumosa, commonly known as the Calder's primrose, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Himalayas. This perennial herb is used in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Primula calderiana var. strumosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe coughs, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy for respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
